result:
Fixed. I left the `images` field out entirely (rather than pointing it at another guessed path) since no OG asset exists in `public/` — that's a real asset someone needs to create, not something I should fabricate a path for.

## Prioritized action plan

| # | Issue | Owner | Effort |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Metadata/OG mismatch | ✅ Fixed here | Done |
| 2 | Create & wire a real OG image at `/public/img/` | Design/dev | Small |
| 3 | Rebuild `/blog/[slug]` route + `/api/posts` (or static content) so the 12 already-ranking post URLs serve real content again, and add them (and future posts) to `sitemap.ts` | Dev + content | Medium-large — highest traffic upside in this audit |
| 4 | Either build real FAQ content at `#faq` on the homepage, or drop the `/faq` nav link and the dead redirect | Dev + content | Small |
| 5 | Confirm `/features` and `/pricing` actually resolve live — this is the site's only conversion path and I couldn't verify it this session | Dev | Verify ASAP |
| 6 | Once the pivot is reflected everywhere, start building non-brand content/targeting toward meeting-notes intent (currently zero footprint in GSC) — the legacy blog's email-cleanup topics won't carry into the new positioning | Content/SEO | Ongoing |

The single highest-leverage fix was #1, which is done. #3 is the biggest remaining opportunity since those URLs already carry real search demand that's currently being thrown away.
